stimulus:
Welcome.

In this experiment we will ask you to imagine people's motivations for saying sentences.

Each trial will begin with a sentence that somebody might say to describe an event involving two different people.

For instance, someone might say Harry is being recruited by Molly

We want you to think about which of those two people (Molly and Harry) the speaker identifies more with. For instance, why did the speaker say Harry is being recruited by Molly rather than Molly is recruiting Harry? Might that mean that the speaker is socially closer to one of the two characters?

After you have read the sentence, you will rate on a scale which of the two characters the speaker identifies more with.

You will give your answer by clicking a button on a rating scale.

Sometimes there will also be catch questions designed to make sure that you are paying attention, which ask about a different topic.

To do this task, you will have to use your imagination to think about and visualise the events, and how the speaker might relate to the characters.


Ready?
sentence_type: NA

stimulus: Before the experiment starts, we will show you an example of how you will answer the questions. Press 'Continue'.
